rulings.Case Title: FISCAL FUNDING CO. INC. VS. ALLEN MATKIS LEEK GAMBRE MALLORY LLP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Attorneys Fees Incurred On Appeal And In Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 25, 2015, Line 1, RESPONDENT ALAN DONES, JOHN GUILLORY Motion For Attorneys Fees Incurred On Appeal And In Arbitration. Defendant's Motion For Attorneys Fees Incurred On Appeal And In Arbitration is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. This Court has determined that respondents are contractually entitled to attorneys' fees and costs under the operating agreement between the parties. Respondents are the prevailing parties in both the second appeal and the arbitration. This court has already held that the award of attorneys' fees should be against both plaintiff Fiscal Funding and its alter ego, Calvin Grigsby. This court, however, previously awarded attorneys' fees and costs to Defendant in 2011, 2012, 2014 and 2015. Defendant seeks $364,463 in additional attorneys' fees for 2008 through 2015. Defendants do not establish with sufficient detail whether the attorneys' fees that Defendants presently seek are separate from the attorneys' fees this court already awarded. This court will not grant Defendant's motion for additional attorneys' fees and costs absent a showing that there is no overlap with this Court's previous orders granting attorneys' fees and costs to Defendant. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/SB)

rulings.Case Title: ELDON HODGIN VS. DIRECTOR OF THE DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Application For Verified Petition For Writ Of Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 25, 2015, Line 2, PETITIONER ELDON HODGIN'S Application For Verified Petition For Writ Of Mandate. DENIED. To comply with California's implied consent law, "a driver should clearly and unambiguously manifest the consent required by the law. Consent which is not clear and unambiguous may be deemed a refusal." (Garcia v. Department of Motor Vehicles (2010) 185 Cal.App.4th 73, 82, quoting Carrey v. Department of Motor Vehicles (1986) 183 Cal.App.3d 1265, 1270.) Here, the record demonstrates that Petitioner repeatedly refused to consent to treatment before making the ambiguous statement that "Your [sic] going to take my blood anyway so what's the point." The administrative hearing officer was entitled to conclude that Hodgin refused to submit to a chemical test, and correctly imposed the one-year suspension. Prevailing party to submit a judgment in accordance with this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/SB)

rulings.Case Title: JULIE FAUSTINO VS. PG&E CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Tax Defendant Pges Memorandum Of Costs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 25, 2015, Line 7, PLAINTIFF JULIE FAUSTINO'S Motion To Tax Defendant PG&E's Memorandum Of Costs. Plaintiff's Motion to Tax Costs of PG&E is DENIED. Deposition subpoena fees for healthcare providers are recoverable costs. (See Naser v. Lakeridge Athletic Club (2014) 227 Cal.App.4th 571, 576-78.) Plaintiff does not provide any support for her assertion that PG&Es deposition costs are outrageous and unreasonable. Plaintiff fails to carry her burden to show these costs are unreasonable. (See Nelson v. Anderson (1999) 72 Cal.App.4th 111, 131.) Costs of $7,176.85 awarded. rulings.Case Title: ARKADY ZELIKOVSKY et al VS. 3520 BALBOA STREET OWNERS ASSOCIATION, A et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Productions Of Documents And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, August 25, 2015, Line 2, PLAINTIFF ARKADY ZELIKOVSKY AND ELENA ZELIKOVSKY'S Motion To Compel Productions Of Documents And For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Noah Lebowitz, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties to appear. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to noah@dplolaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ROSEMARY THAKER VS. POTRERO HEALTHE CENTER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 25, 2015, Line 10, DEFENDANT ANDREA NATTA, L.A.C'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff's cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress fails because Plaintiff does not plead conduct so "extreme as to exceed all bounds of that usually tolerated in a civilized society." (Hughes v. Pair (2009) 36 Cal.4th 1035, 1050-51.) Even accepting Plaintiff's allegations that Defendant Natta left her alone for 1 hour while performing acupuncture treatment does not rise to the level of intentional infliction of emotional distress. Plaintiff was given a previous opportunity to amend to show outrageous behavior, and has not done so. rulings.Case Title: ARKADY BERGER VS. GARY VARUM ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order Precluding Or Limiting The Deposition Of Irina Varum Aka Irene Varum
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, August 25, 2015, Line 6, DEFENDANTS GARY VARUM, ALEX VARUM, NATALIE KREIGEL, AND IRINA VARUM'S Motion For Protective Order Precluding Or Limiting The Deposition Of Irina Varum Aka Irene Varum. Pro Tem Judge Noah Lebowitz, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion for Protective Order is DENIED. The deponent is a named defendant in this case. The doctor's letter submitted by the moving party is vague and does not establish that the deponent is completely unable to testify or that there are no accommodations that can be made to enable her to testify. The moving party's declarations consist of second-hand statements or statements made without sufficiently explaining the basis for the personal knowledge to make such statements, or improper opinion testimony. Counsel for the moving party did not engage in good faith meet and confer prior to filing this motion. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is GRANTED in the amount of $1,625.00, payable within 10 days of notice of entry of order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to noah@dplolaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: WARREN LEGARIE VS. MAHMOUD ASSAF ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion And Notice Of Hearing To Compel Further Responses To Document Requests; Request For Monetary Sanction Of $ 4,672 Against Defendant Talab Qarajah
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 25, 2015, Line 11, PLAINTIFF WARREN LEGARIE'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Document Requests; Request For Monetary Sanction Of $ 4,672 Against Defendant Talab Qarajah. Plaintiff's motion is granted in part and denied in part as follows: 1. The motion is granted in part as to request numbers 1, 2, 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, 12, and 13. (a) As to requests numbers 1, 7, and 8, responding party's response is incomplete. The statement that "the category does not exist" is not an acceptable response. Responding party is to submit an amended statement in response within ten (10) days stating whether responsive documents exist or will be produced. Any potential objection as to ambiguity is overruled. (b) With reference to requests numbers 2, 9 - 13, the privacy objection is well taken except as to any leasehold documents that reflect a transfer of personal property that had belonged to Assaf or SF Gondola Inc. ("Assaf's personal property"), such as the lease, a schedule of property included with the lease, or a bill of sale for personal property. Defendant is to provide an amended response and produce within ten (10) days any leasehold documents or other documents reflecting a transfer of Assaf's personal property from Qarajah. 2. The motion is denied as to numbers 3, 4, 5, and 6. 3. The request for sanctions is denied. The objections are found to have been substantially justified. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/SB)

Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/SB)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL A MAYCHROWITZ VS. GAORONG CHEN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Production Of Documents, To Compel Further Answers To Interrogatories And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, August 25, 2015, Line 7, PLAINTIFF PAUL MAYCHROWITZ'S Motion To Compel Production Of Documents, To Compel Further Answers To Interrogatories And For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Noah Lebowitz, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion to compel further responses to interrogatories and document requests is GRANTED. In regard to Form Interrogatory No. 12.2, the Defendant's answers was stated in general terms and failed answer the subquestions. In regard to Form Interrogatory No. 12.3, again the Defendant failed to answer any of the subquestions. In regard to Request for Production No. 10, the Defendant did not interpose any objections. Based on the meet and confer correspondence, there appears to be responsive documents which have not been produced. The Plaintiff's request for sanctions is GRANTED in the amount of $1,900, payable within 10 days of notice of entry of order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to noah@dplolaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
